sourceMovie.title: Dust Bunny
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dust Bunny (2025) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Bryan Fuller's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mortal
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dust Bunny clicked for you, Mortal (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Look Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dust Bunny clicked for you, Look Away (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dust Bunny?
faq.answer: Try Mortal, Look Away, The Killer—each shares DNA with Dust Bunny while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dust Bunny?
faq.answer: Dust Bunny (2025) is best described as Fantasy, Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dust Bunny worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
